title: Savory Rava Upma
description: Fluffy semolina upma with tempering, vegetables, and lemon. Reliable breakfast or rainy-day tea-time bowl.
explanation: Upma is the South-Indian-leaning quick meal that uses pantry rava and a simple tadka. Vegetables stretch it into a fuller plate; lemon and coriander keep it lively.
The main skill is toasting rava and using the right water ratio so it stays fluffy, not sticky.
instructions: Dry-roast rava on medium heat until aromatic and light golden. Set aside.
Heat oil. Add mustard, urad, chana dal, curry leaves, and chilli. Saute until dals turn golden.
Add onion and vegetables; cook until soft.
Add water and salt; bring to a rolling boil.
Lower heat, pour roasted rava in a steady stream while stirring to avoid lumps.
Cover and cook 2–3 minutes. Fluff, add lemon and coriander, serve hot.
stepNotes: Roasting rava is non-negotiable for good flavor and non-gummy texture.
Pour rava slowly into boiling water while stirring continuously.
Water ratio ~2.5:1 for soft upma; use 2:1 for firmer texture.
Skip onion for no-onion versions; hing and extra curry leaves help.
A spoon of grated coconut on top is excellent if you like it.

variationSections.title: Grain alternatives
items: Broken wheat (dalia) upma for more fiber.
Millet rava upma for a gluten-free direction (use gluten-free rava).
Oats upma for a modern breakfast twist.

variationSections.title: Protein add-ins
items: Mix in crumbled tofu or cooked moong at the end.
Serve with a side of plant curd or sprout salad.
